# PROTOTYPE_QA.md — Prototype Generation Quality Protocol

> **Created 2026-06-22 after Craftmans Cafe incident.**
> A prototype with leaked AI prompt text and 1×1 pixel fake images was pushed to GitHub and deployed to Vercel. This must never happen again.

---

## The Incident

**What happened:** Craftmans Cafe prototype (proto-craftmans-cafe) was generated by MiniMax M3 and pushed without any QA. The HTML contained:
1. The model's internal reasoning/thinking text leaked as literal page content (a wall of text before the actual page)
2. Image files were 1×1 pixel transparent PNGs (68 bytes) instead of real images
3. No visual verification was done before pushing

**Root causes:**
- No QA gate between generation and push
- No image generation step (placeholders used instead)
- No browser rendering check
- No file size validation on assets

**Impact:** Broken prototype visible on live site and dashboard. Unprofessional. Would destroy credibility with any prospect who saw it.

---

## Mandatory QA Protocol (BEFORE every prototype push)

### Step 1: HTML Cleanliness Check
- [ ] Read the generated `index.html` file
- [ ] Confirm NO AI reasoning/thinking text leaked into the HTML
- [ ] Confirm NO prompt instructions appear as visible page text
- [ ] Confirm the first line of the body is actual HTML content (not model commentary)
- [ ] If ANY leaked text found: strip it, regenerate, or manually clean

### Step 2: Image Asset Validation
- [ ] Every image file referenced in the HTML must exist
- [ ] Every image file must be > 5KB (reject 1×1 placeholders)
- [ ] Every image file must be a valid image format (JPEG/PNG/WebP, not text/HTML)
- [ ] Run: `file data/prototypes/<slug>/images/*` — verify output says "JPEG" or "PNG image data" with reasonable dimensions
- [ ] If any image is missing or a placeholder: generate real images with `image_generate` BEFORE pushing

### Step 3: Browser Rendering Check
- [ ] Open the prototype in the browser tool (local or deployed)
- [ ] Take a screenshot
- [ ] Visually confirm: no broken images, no leaked text, proper layout, professional appearance
- [ ] If it looks wrong: STOP, fix, re-check. Do not push.

### Step 4: Screenshot Capture
- [ ] Use Playwright to capture desktop + mobile screenshots
- [ ] Save to `data/prototypes/<slug>/screenshot.png`
- [ ] Update `prototypes.json` with the screenshot path
- [ ] Verify screenshot file is > 10KB (not a blank/empty capture)

### Step 5: Git Push Gate
- [ ] All above checks pass
- [ ] `tsc --noEmit` clean (if any TS files changed)
- [ ] `next build` succeeds (if app files changed)
- [ ] Commit with descriptive message
- [ ] Push to GitHub
- [ ] Verify Vercel deployment succeeds
- [ ] Open the live URL and confirm it renders correctly

---

## Prototype Generation Workflow (step by step)

1. **Read relevant skills first** — consult `frontend` SKILL.md (and `nextjs-expert`/`react`/`typescripts-mastery`/`ia-tailwind-css` if applicable) for patterns, typography, color systems, and best practices before writing any HTML/CSS
2. **Generate HTML with MiniMax M3** (not by writing it manually) — pass the lead info + industry context + business branding details so the output feels personalized
3. **Clean the HTML** — strip any model reasoning, thinking blocks, or leaked prompts
4. **Apply target business branding** — add the actual business name, address, phone, and a color theme/aesthetic that fits the industry. The prototype should look like THEIRS, not a generic template
5. **Identify all image references** in the HTML
6. **Generate real images** for each reference using `image_generate` (OpenAI/Gemini)
7. **Save images** to `data/prototypes/<slug>/images/` with correct filenames
8. **Validate images** (file size, format, dimensions — see Step 2 above)
9. **Open in browser** — visual QA check (see Step 3 above)
10. **Capture screenshot** with Playwright
11. **Update prototypes.json** with screenshot path + status
12. **Commit + push** only after all checks pass
13. **Verify live deployment**

---

## What NOT to do

- ❌ Never push a prototype without seeing it rendered in a browser
- ❌ Never use 1×1 pixel placeholder images
- ❌ Never push HTML that contains AI model reasoning/thinking text
- ❌ Never skip the screenshot step
- ❌ Never mark `generation_status: "completed"` without visual verification
- ❌ Never push to GitHub without running all QA checks above

---

## File Structure (standard for every prototype)

```
data/prototypes/<business-slug>/
├── index.html              ← clean, no leaked text
├── screenshot.png          ← real Playwright screenshot (>10KB)
└── images/
    ├── hero.jpg            ← real image (>5KB)
    ├── section_1.jpg       ← real image (>5KB)
    └── section_2.jpg       ← real image (>5KB)
```

---

## Reminder

This protocol exists because a prototype was pushed with:
- AI thinking text visible on the page
- 1×1 pixel fake images

It was seen by the user on the live dashboard. It was embarrassing and unprofessional. **This must never happen again.**
